mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 10 percent, severe labor market slack signals collapsing consumer income and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 10.0, or the signal loses predictive power with p-value greater than 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which overrides supply chain constraints and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts short-term equity valuations when monetary policy remains accommodative.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during periods of active Fed tightening, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS rises above 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 255 signal persistent inflationary pressure that forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 255.0 coincides with positive forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or inflation fails to predict Fed rate hikes within 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns with Rank IC below 0.01, or positive returns occur in less than 45 percent of months with payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate profit margins, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter when financial conditions remain stable.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive returns during periods of credit market stress, or the signal loses significance when VIX exceeds 30.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signals synchronized supply-side recovery, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.
